question: [File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an attacker exploit the TakeAmountsLib.buyerAssetsToUnits rounding to cause filledBuyerAssets to exceed targetFilledBuyerAssets, causing the require(filledBuyerAssets == targetFilledBuyerAssets) check to fail even though the attacker filled exactly the right number of units, under the precondition that buyerPrice is not a divisor of targetFilledBuyerAssets, triggering the sequence: attacker calls bundler -> buyerAssetsToUnits computes units -> take yields filledBuyerAssets != targetFilledBuyerAssets due to mulDivUp rounding -> require fails -> OutOfOffers revert, violating the invariant that the bundler must be able to fill exactly targetFilledBuyerAssets for any valid input, causing scoped impact: DoS of asset-targeted buy bundle due to rounding? Proof idea: fuzz targetBuyerAssets and offer tick and assert filledBuyerAssets == targetFilledBuyerAssets after successful loop.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget / supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget] Can an attacker who is authorized by taker supply collateral at a collateralIndex that is not in the market's collateralParams, causing an out-of-bounds access in Midnight.supplyCollateral, under the precondition that the attacker passes collateralSupplies[i].collateralIndex >= market.collateralParams.length, triggering the sequence: attacker calls bundler -> bundler calls supplyCollateral(market, collateralIndex=999, assets, taker) -> Midnight accesses market.collateralParams[999] -> revert with array OOB, violating the invariant that collateral index must be validated before use, causing scoped impact: DoS of supply+sell bundle? Proof idea: unit test with out-of-bounds collateralIndex and assert revert.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an attacker exploit the ConsumableUnitsLib.consumableUnits() call to manipulate the units taken per offer by front-running a setConsumed() call that changes the consumed amount between the consumableUnits() view call and the actual take(), under the precondition that the attacker can call setConsumed on behalf of the maker (if authorized) between the bundler's view call and the take, triggering the sequence: bundler calls consumableUnits() -> attacker front-runs with setConsumed(group, newAmount, maker) -> bundler calls take with stale unitsToTake -> take uses updated consumed -> take may revert with ConsumedUnits or fill less than expected -> filledUnits < targetUnits -> OutOfOffers revert, violating the invariant that consumableUnits must be consistent with the actual take execution, causing scoped impact: DoS of buy bundle via front-run consumed manipulation? Proof idea: unit test simulating front-run setConsumed between consumableUnits and take.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: repayAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an attacker call repayAndWithdrawCollateral with a Permit2 TokenPermit where the permit amount is less than `assets`, causing the Permit2 transfer to succeed for a smaller amount but the subsequent safeTransferFrom to fail, under the precondition that the permit.kind == PermitKind.Permit2 and the permit amount < assets, triggering the sequence: attacker calls bundler -> pullToken with Permit2 -> IPermit2.permitTransferFrom with amount=permitAmount < assets -> Permit2 transfers only permitAmount -> bundler has insufficient balance -> forceApproveMax -> repay(market, units=assets-referralFeeAssets, ...) -> Midnight pulls units from bundler -> bundler has insufficient balance -> revert, violating the invariant that Permit2 permit amount must match the requested pull amount, causing scoped impact: DoS or partial repay with incorrect accounting? Proof idea: unit test with Permit2 permit amount < assets and assert revert.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an attacker pass collateralWithdrawals with collateralIndex values that are not in the taker's activated collateral bitmap, causing withdrawCollateral to succeed (withdrawing 0 assets) or fail, under the precondition that the taker has no collateral at the specified index, triggering the sequence: attacker calls bundler -> takes succeed -> bundler calls withdrawCollateral(market, collateralIndex=5, assets=0, taker, collateralReceiver) -> Midnight processes 0-asset withdrawal -> no-op or bitmap manipulation, violating the invariant that collateral withdrawal must only affect activated collateral slots, causing scoped impact: collateral bitmap corruption or no-op withdrawal? Proof idea: unit test with collateralIndex not in taker's bitmap and assets=0, assert bitmap unchanged.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget] Can an attacker exploit the fact that the bundler calls supplyCollateral(market, collateralIndex, assets, taker) where msg.sender to Midnight is the bundler (not the attacker), causing Midnight to check isAuthorized(taker, bundler) instead of isAuthorized(taker, attacker), under the precondition that taker has authorized the bundler but NOT the attacker, triggering the sequence: attacker calls bundler -> bundler checks isAuthorized(taker, attacker) at line 127 -> passes if attacker is authorized -> bundler calls supplyCollateral with msg.sender=bundler -> Midnight checks isAuthorized(taker, bundler) -> passes if bundler is authorized -> collateral supplied, violating the invariant that the authorization check in the bundler must be equivalent to the authorization check in Midnight for the same operation, causing scoped impact: attacker can supply collateral to victim's position if attacker is authorized by victim but bundler is not? Proof idea: unit test where attacker is authorized but bundler is not, assert supplyCollateral reverts in Midnight.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an attacker exploit the settlement fee change between touchMarket() and the actual take() to cause the bundler to overpay or underpay for units, under the precondition that the fee setter changes the settlement fee between the bundler's touchMarket call and the take execution (in the same block via multicall), triggering the sequence: attacker calls bundler -> touchMarket called -> fee setter changes settlement fee -> take executes with new fee -> buyerAssets computed with new fee -> filledBuyerAssets differs from expected -> maxBuyerAssets - filledBuyerAssets - referralFeeAssets may underflow or leave excess, violating the invariant that the settlement fee used in touchMarket must be consistent with the fee used in take, causing scoped impact: incorrect token distribution or underflow in refund calculation? Proof idea: unit test with fee change between touchMarket and take, assert refund is correct.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget] Can an attacker exploit the two-step referral fee formula (referralFeeAssets = targetSellerAssets * referralFeePct / (WAD - referralFeePct)) to cause targetFilledSellerAssets = targetSellerAssets + referralFeeAssets to be less than targetSellerAssets when referralFeePct=0, and verify that the bundler correctly sends targetSellerAssets to receiver even when filledSellerAssets == targetFilledSellerAssets == targetSellerAssets, under the precondition that referralFeePct=0, triggering the sequence: attacker calls bundler with referralFeePct=0 -> referralFeeAssets=0 -> targetFilledSellerAssets=targetSellerAssets -> loop fills exactly targetSellerAssets -> bundler sends targetSellerAssets to receiver -> no referral fee sent, violating the invariant that zero referral fee must result in receiver getting exactly targetSellerAssets, causing scoped impact: accounting verification for zero-fee case? Proof idea: unit test with referralFeePct=0 and assert receiver gets exactly targetSellerAssets.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an attacker pass takes where takes[i].offer.buy=true (buy offer) instead of false (sell offer), causing the InconsistentSide check to revert, but only after the bundler has already pulled maxBuyerAssets from msg.sender and approved MIDNIGHT, under the precondition that the attacker passes a mix of buy and sell offers, triggering the sequence: attacker calls bundler -> pullToken pulls maxBuyerAssets -> forceApproveMax -> loop starts -> require(!takes[i].offer.buy) fails for buy offer -> revert -> maxBuyerAssets stuck in bundler? No, revert undoes all state, violating the invariant that revert must fully undo all state changes including token pulls, causing scoped impact: verify atomicity of revert with pre-loop token pull? Proof idea: unit test with mixed offer sides and assert full revert including token balance.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget] Can an attacker exploit the fact that the bundler calls take(..., taker, receiverIfTakerIsSeller=address(this), ...) where the bundler itself is the receiver, and then transfers filledSellerAssets to the caller-controlled `receiver` parameter, to redirect loan assets to an attacker-controlled address even when taker != msg.sender, under the precondition that attacker is authorized by taker and passes receiver=attacker, triggering the sequence: attacker calls bundler -> bundler checks isAuthorized(taker, attacker) -> passes -> bundler calls take with receiverIfTakerIsSeller=address(this) -> loan assets flow to bundler -> bundler transfers to receiver=attacker, violating the invariant that loan assets from selling must only reach the taker or taker-designated receiver, causing scoped impact: unauthorized loan asset redirection to attacker? Proof idea: fuzz receiver address and assert loan assets only reach taker-approved addresses.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral / buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an attacker exploit the fact that the bundler calls take(..., taker, receiverIfTakerIsSeller=address(0), ...) for buy-side takes, causing the seller's assets to go to address(0) (burned) if the offer's receiverIfMakerIsSeller is address(0), under the precondition that the offer has receiverIfMakerIsSeller=address(0) and the taker is the buyer, triggering the sequence: attacker calls bundler -> bundler calls take with receiverIfTakerIsSeller=address(0) -> Midnight computes receiver = offer.receiverIfMakerIsSeller = address(0) -> sellerAssets transferred to address(0) -> burned, violating the invariant that seller assets must never be sent to address(0), causing scoped impact: permanent loss of seller's loan assets? Proof idea: unit test with offer.receiverIfMakerIsSeller=address(0) and assert sellerAssets not burned.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: repayAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an attacker exploit the fact that repay() in Midnight checks isAuthorized(onBehalf, msg.sender) where msg.sender is the bundler, but the bundler's authorization check uses isAuthorized(onBehalf, msg.sender) where msg.sender is the attacker, creating a two-level authorization check that could be inconsistent, under the precondition that onBehalf has authorized the bundler but the bundler has NOT been authorized by onBehalf for repay (impossible since authorization is coarse-grained), triggering the sequence: attacker calls bundler -> bundler checks isAuthorized(onBehalf, attacker) -> passes -> bundler calls repay(market, units, onBehalf, address(0), '') -> Midnight checks isAuthorized(onBehalf, bundler) -> if bundler not authorized, revert, violating the invariant that bundler authorization check must be sufficient to guarantee Midnight authorization check passes, causing scoped impact: DoS of repay bundle if bundler not authorized by onBehalf? Proof idea: unit test where attacker is authorized but bundler is not, assert repay reverts in Midnight.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an attacker exploit the market struct passed in takes[0].offer.market to create a new market (via touchMarket) with a malicious enterGate that always returns true for canIncreaseCredit but reverts for canIncreaseDebt, under the precondition that the attacker crafts a market with a malicious enterGate contract, triggering the sequence: attacker calls bundler -> touchMarket creates new market with malicious enterGate -> take executes with buyer=taker (credit increase) -> enterGate.canIncreaseCredit returns true -> take succeeds -> collateralWithdrawals executed -> taker gains credit without proper gate validation, violating the invariant that enter gate must properly restrict credit increases, causing scoped impact: gate bypass allowing unauthorized credit increase? Proof idea: mock malicious enterGate and assert gate is called correctly for all credit/debt increases.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget] Can an attacker exploit the fact that collateralSupplies are processed using market.collateralParams[collateralSupplies[i].collateralIndex].token to determine the token address, where the market struct is taken from takes[0].offer.market (caller-controlled), to supply a different token than intended, under the precondition that the attacker crafts takes[0].offer.market with different collateralParams than the actual market, triggering the sequence: attacker calls bundler -> market = takes[0].offer.market with crafted collateralParams -> token = market.collateralParams[collateralIndex].token = maliciousToken -> pullToken(maliciousToken, attacker, assets, permit) -> supplyCollateral(market, collateralIndex, assets, taker) -> Midnight uses actual market's collateralParams -> mismatch, violating the invariant that the token pulled must match the token expected by Midnight for the given collateral index, causing scoped impact: wrong token supplied as collateral or accounting mismatch? Proof idea: unit test with crafted market collateralParams and assert token mismatch causes revert.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an attacker exploit the try/catch around take() to silently skip a take that fails due to a ratifier rejection, then fill the remaining units with a different (potentially malicious) offer, under the precondition that the attacker includes a mix of valid and invalid offers in the takes array, triggering the sequence: attacker calls bundler -> first take fails (ratifier rejects) -> silently skipped -> second take with different offer fills remaining units -> filledUnits == targetUnits -> collateralWithdrawals executed -> taker's position modified by unexpected offer, violating the invariant that the bundler must only fill offers that the taker explicitly approved, causing scoped impact: unauthorized take execution via offer substitution after ratifier failure? Proof idea: unit test with first offer failing ratifier and second offer succeeding, assert only intended offers are filled.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an attacker exploit the fact that the bundler does not validate that collateralWithdrawals[i].collateralIndex is within the bounds of market.collateralParams, causing an out-of-bounds access in Midnight.withdrawCollateral, under the precondition that the attacker passes collateralWithdrawals with collateralIndex >= market.collateralParams.length, triggering the sequence: attacker calls bundler -> takes succeed -> bundler calls withdrawCollateral(market, collateralIndex=999, assets, taker, collateralReceiver) -> Midnight accesses market.collateralParams[999] -> revert with array OOB, violating the invariant that collateral index must be validated before use in withdrawCollateral, causing scoped impact: DoS of buy+withdraw bundle? Proof idea: unit test with out-of-bounds collateralIndex in collateralWithdrawals and assert revert.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget] Can an attacker exploit the require(filledUnits <= maxUnits) check to cause a DoS by crafting offers where sellerAssetsToUnits returns more units than maxUnits, under the precondition that the attacker controls the offer tick and the target seller assets, triggering the sequence: attacker calls bundler with maxUnits=1 -> sellerAssetsToUnits computes units=2 for targetFilledSellerAssets -> take fills 2 units -> filledUnits=2 > maxUnits=1 -> require fails -> UnitsTooHigh revert, violating the invariant that the bundler must be able to fill targetSellerAssets without exceeding maxUnits for any valid offer configuration, causing scoped impact: DoS of sell bundle via maxUnits constraint? Proof idea: fuzz maxUnits and offer tick and assert UnitsTooHigh is only triggered when genuinely too many units are needed.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an attacker exploit the fact that the bundler calls take() with takerCallback=address(0) and takerCallbackData='', preventing the taker from using a callback to source funds atomically, while the bundler itself holds the loan tokens, under the precondition that the taker intended to use a callback to source funds but the bundler hardcodes address(0) for takerCallback, triggering the sequence: attacker calls bundler -> bundler calls take(offer, ratifierData, units, taker, address(0), address(0), '') -> no taker callback -> payer = msg.sender (bundler) -> bundler must have sufficient loan tokens pre-pulled -> if bundler has insufficient balance, take reverts, violating the invariant that the bundler must always have sufficient loan tokens to pay for all takes, causing scoped impact: DoS of buy bundle if maxBuyerAssets is insufficient for all takes? Proof idea: unit test where total buyerAssets across all takes exceeds maxBuyerAssets and assert OutOfOffers or insufficient balance revert.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: repayAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an attacker exploit the fact that repay() reduces debt before withdrawCollateral() checks health, to withdraw more collateral than would be allowed before repayment, under the precondition that the taker has a position where debt reduction improves health enough to allow additional collateral withdrawal, triggering the sequence: attacker calls bundler -> repay reduces debt -> health improves -> withdrawCollateral withdraws collateral that was previously locked -> collateral sent to collateralReceiver=attacker, violating the invariant that collateral withdrawal must only be authorized by the position owner, causing scoped impact: authorized operator can drain collateral after forced repayment? Proof idea: unit test where repay enables collateral withdrawal that was previously blocked, assert collateralReceiver is taker-controlled.
